name: The White House
subject: Resolute Reads
body:
1600 Daily REAL NEWS PRESIDENT TRUMP DOESN'T WANT YOU TO MISS

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=111c0998bbb0deaf964b0fc0ac7ae1ff08083f4bb68ff52def2f4e362be06e8ee2138faa027e64eac882bf3598e08586e605b233abf2aec3
Almost 300 MS-13 Gang Members Arrested in Nationwide Bust

- The Washington Free Beacon

Under the leadership of President Trump, the Department of Justice, and the FBI, “Operation: Raging Bull” led to the arrest of nearly 300 members and associates of the infamous transnational gang MS-13, making the country safer by taking these criminals off the streets for good.

-------------------------

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=629292329b977aa92c0b2a0c2769b5465610d94fdbc35efb6263d77cb20a2764e13e700ef49f61a7c771539fed1d46c13fe4997fb1c98267
On Tax Reform, This Communications Giant Puts $1B Where Its Mouth Is

- The Daily Signal

Last week AT&T announced it "has committed to investing $1 billion in the economy if Congress cuts corporate taxes." With these tax cuts—combined with the President's efforts to cut job-killing regulations—our country is poised for historic economic growth.

-------------------------

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=629292329b977aa9fb46fab2452c08b0ac4cbb20186381ab1e9144278dbf90b526e089b2ea9d764059e6ec287d48625b4de90bbb1bb5f3c5
Republicans Just Took Another Huge Step Forward on Their Tax-Cut Plan

- Business Insider

On Thursday evening, the Senate Finance Committee passed the Tax Cuts and Jobs Act "advancing it to the Senate floor for consideration by the full chamber."

-------------------------

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=629292329b977aa93050b807bae0f75407558dc4b90c375fc78d59cbce8bd094b86b0373c7e9e2a14dedf4b42d538d6f7e15a8ca173fbe39
House Passes Its Version of Tax Reform

- The New York Post

The House easily passed “a sweeping Republican tax bill that cuts taxes for corporations and many people.” The House voted 227-205 to approve the bill, and the victory advances a hopeful end-of-the-year legislative achievement for President Trump.

-------------------------

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=629292329b977aa9bbfd9a31f2a9186329fe6e5dbefd24f4fd4e040967555b3c4975dc11a446819561f7812fce85cd1386a6671115a40618
President Trump Prays with Oklahoma Softball Team on Day NCAA Champs Visit White House

- The New York Daily News

On Friday, President Trump welcomed 18 NCAA championship teams to the White House and congratulated them on their success. At one point during the event “joined by Education Secretary Betsy DeVos, Trump bowed his head in prayer along with members of the Oklahoma softball team.”

-------------------------

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=629292329b977aa9ec7b87ad87e5c309d7106a6267ffca944d09df2be2027fd3b79f22e113fff1784298e20ad569ca68d8d7caedd9b20779
3 UCLA Basketball Players Thank Donald Trump, Apologize for Shoplifting in China

- Associated Press

After the President intervened on their behalf with China's President Xi, the three UCLA basketball players who were detained in China thanked the President for his efforts to help them come home.

-------------------------

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=629292329b977aa93a8586833541dc7e9a564a521f56ce51079ae277c282ae41f68e75ed06272de112e9fafb9a2a7e444990e69a8b165557
Commerce Secretary Ross: U.S. Has Leverage to Pressure Mexico, Canada in NAFTA Talks

- The Wall Street Journal

In New York City, Commerce Secretary Wilbur Ross talked about the tough ongoing NAFTA negotiations, suggesting the “U.S. can pressure Mexico and Canada into big concessions because they have more to lose if the pact collapses.”

-------------------------

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=629292329b977aa928ef8bcf94ef9b0f9e53de9cff8c2a7367559305e1ce99970b34c9dd9be272b1cd63250cf6997db300678fd7b3257df8
Report Shows Decrease in Opioid-Related Deaths in Massachusetts

- CBS Boston

“Just a few weeks after President Trump declared a public emergency with the opioid crisis,” a new report from the Massachusetts Department of Public Health shows a decrease in opioid-related overdose deaths across the state.

-------------------------

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=629292329b977aa9fc8fad1a6272e59b4fccf3550da751f3e33e6abcbf19807f09aa1b8f49983e6f6914ecacedb7bbb2bab947e6c871d61d
Pence Trying to Fast-Track U.S. Help to Iraqi Christians, Yazidis, Other Religious Minorities

- The Washington Free Beacon

In a White House meeting with top senior administration officials and diplomats, Vice President Pence promised that “help is on the way” for persecuted “Christians, Yazidis and other religious minorities in Iraq who are victims of genocide perpetuated by the Islamic State.”

-------------------------

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=629292329b977aa97ab15f0efc87d00cb5ed37cbf10dfe9ecd30ec016e7f5ce928cd48737baaecb970cbbd4e08c2e9956eabedcbed9fa470
Airstrikes Kill Four Senior ISIS Leaders

- The Washington Examiner

After President Trump’s vow to strike ISIS “10 times harder” following recent attacks, four senior Islamic State leaders have been killed over the past three weeks.

-------------------------

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=629292329b977aa9bb8cb4e7e2e24e735ecc8f8d0e5bdf74678fe3376c1b7755460583b624828083bdc86775f250eb72aa349984925f7f6b
ICE: 25 Arrested in Crackdown on Long Island

- Newsday

In New York, Immigration and Customs Enforcement arrested 25 people sought for deportation, 24 of which had prior convictions for operation of a motor vehicle while drunk or impaired by drugs.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=629292329b977aa9239dc98232c9a1ac5ac2fbb4feae99432bc9b885ca9f66faf17a14d01379b4d332f3d7df23289f722ac093702201b833
Privacy Policy |

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=629292329b977aa91031461edb60a5c58dc6e42cddccea9f0e811d426feecb3d1e8182558e271a3017fa03f5721aaaa66c18327b0b2c8302
Contact the White House |

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/unsub_center.aspx?qs=d453c42a6560d1d466399cc77e7fe5e4ba9a2904767ca5474d169b95ade2fe12fc847c13d44952383fd950e9b61687478742935651332d28
Unsubscribe

name: Akilah Ensley, Andrew Gillum for Governor
subject: Help
body:
#
Hello,

In 2018, we have the opportunity to rebuild Florida into a state that works for all of us -- but that's only going to happen with your help.

I just took a look at our numbers for the quarter, and I've got to be honest with you, we're behind by $3,100. Andrew asked me to give him an update tomorrow and I don't want to let him down.

**[Can you pitch in $5, or whatever you can afford, to help us stay on track and hit our mid-month goal by Tuesday?](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb325a/9e00690/4e9ecc19/240300f2/1323990770/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/gillum-midquarter-goal?utm_campaign=em17_171118_MQuart_fnd_gq_ful__AE&utm_medium=email&utm_source=Gillum&refcode=em17_171118_MQuart_fnd_gq_ful__AE&utm_content=1+-+Can+you+pitch+in+5+or+whatever+you+can+a")**

As Governor, Andrew will create good-paying jobs that support families, protect and expand all Floridians' access to affordable healthcare, and strengthen our public schools. We have the opportunity to show just how strong Floridians can be and elect a Governor who represents us, and not the powerful few.

We have the power to create the change we want for Florida, but Andrew can't do any of this without you.

**[Pitch in $5 now, or whatever you can afford, and help us raise the $3,100 we need to meet our goal by Tuesday.](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb325a/9e00690/4e9ecc19/240300f5/1323990770/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/gillum-midquarter-goal?utm_campaign=em17_171118_MQuart_fnd_gq_ful__AE&utm_medium=email&utm_source=Gillum&refcode=em17_171118_MQuart_fnd_gq_ful__AE&utm_content=2+-+Pitch+in+5+now+or+whatever+you+can+affor")**

Thank you,

Akilah Ensley

name: Debbie Wasserman Schultz
subject: We can't change the past. But we can do better.
disclaimer_text: Paid for and Authorized by Debbie Wasserman Schultz for Congress This email was sent to [dpwillis67@gmail
body:
| |
---
| |
---
| For many families, the decision to place an elderly family member in a nursing home is very difficult. You expect nothing but the best -- the best people, the best resources, the best facility -- to ensure your loved one is safe and happy. Unfortunately, nursing homes in Florida and across the nation are not meeting that expectation. When Hurricane Irma barreled through Florida earlier this year, thousands lost power -- including nursing home facilities. Elderly individuals died because of lack of air conditioning. Nursing homes need to treat their residents like people, not dollar signs. They shouldn't cut corners when it comes to things like emergency backup generators. I'm working on a plan that would: 1\. Require nursing homes to have sufficient backup generators to power air conditioning for at least 96 hours.

2\. Require utility companies to make nursing homes a top priority after natural disasters, just like they do with hospitals.

3\. Assist small nursing homes in complying with these requirements. We can't change what happened after Hurricane Irma. But we can make sure this preventable loss of life doesn't happen again. [_**If you support this plan, add your name.**_](https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/page/m/10113e71/622b7b5b/7907a52/307baa1f/438121591/VEsH/ "https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/e171118") Thank you. name: Jim Zogby, Our Revolution
subject: My side of the story
body:
Because I was a superdelegate for Bernie Sanders and supported Keith Ellison for Chair of the Party, I was deeply disappointed, but not surprised
#
Friends,
I began my service as a member of the Democratic National Committee in 1993, after serving as a deputy campaign manager in Reverend Jesse Jackson's two presidential campaigns. I served on the DNC's Executive Committee since 2001 and from 2006 until the recent meeting in Las Vegas, I was also co-chair of the Resolutions Committee. Because I was a superdelegate for Bernie Sanders and supported Keith Ellison for Chair of the Party, I was deeply disappointed, but not surprised, to be replaced as co-chair of Resolutions and removed from the Executive Committee.
What concerns me is that after a hotly contested primary race in 2016 and a close election for DNC chair in 2017, this was the time for Democrats to come together before the important 2018 mid-terms. Instead of seeking unity, the slate nominated by the DNC chair Tom Perez and confirmed by the DNC at the Las Vegas meeting virtually eliminated Sanders and Ellison supporters from the party's major governing bodies.
Keith Ellison is the only one of 11 officers who supported Bernie's nomination. There are virtually no Bernie supporters on the Rules and By-laws Committee and Keith is the only Bernie supporter on the new Executive Committee. We should have more representation, and it is fundamentally wrong that we will have such a diminished voice in party affairs when, more than ever, we will need "all hands on deck."
At times like this, I remember a lesson Jesse taught me a generation ago: "Don't quit, stick around and fight." And so instead of dropping out, I remain committed to staying engaged and continuing the fight to change the Party.
I am encouraged by the number of Our Revolution and other progressives elected to party positions in more than 10 states, including party chairs in several states. And I am hopeful that the Unity Reform Commission, on which I serve as a Sanders appointee, will soon vote on a series of measures to reform both how we, as Democrats, choose our presidential candidates and how we run our party. We are calling for greater budget transparency, accountability, inclusion, and democratic decision-making.
When our reform proposals are sent to the entire DNC for a vote, Our Revolution will work to mobilize Democrats across the nation to join our call for real change--because we believe that the reforms we are proposing are necessary for unity. And, we believe that unity is the prerequisite for victory.
Our Revolution is the largest national organization demanding party reform in each of the state parties as well as nationally. We believe that we must build strong groups through party building inside and sometimes outside the party, and link to strong candidates working with us on key local, state, and national issues.
**[CONTRIBUTE»](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/2ba7ae15/bed43eb/2de7c20b/75ff5d42/1224338594/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/or-dnc?refcode=em171118-s&noskip=true")**
Join me in donating and working actively by joining a group or starting a group in your community. We can't just wait for the next election--we need sustained organizing around our issues, message, candidates, and ballot measures, and a reformed Democratic Party that supports us.
In solidarity,
Dr. Jim Zogby
Our Revolution Board Member

Peter,

We aren't finished with 2017 elections!

Last week, three of our women running in special elections for Georgia state legislative seats earned enough votes to head into runoffs in December.

Want to keep adding to our state and local wins this year?

**[Donate $3 to help elect our three women in Georgia and our state and local candidates across the country.](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c6dfb/24c69fd9/165de43da/1891dcc6/2818168697/VEsE/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/20171117_email_GASpecial?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=2+-+Donate+3+to+help+elect+our+three+women+i&utm_campaign=WQEFEK35N_digital_email_20171118_GASpecial_inact&source=WQEFEK35N_digital_email_20171118_GASpecial_inact")**

We've had our eye on the Georgia legislature ever since we met Stacey Abrams, our first-ever Gabrielle Giffords Rising Star Award winner, who we're excited is now running for governor!

These women will make us just as proud as Stacey has in the Georgia Capitol:
**Jen Jordan** (SD-06) is a publicly educated lawyer who stands up for the people the government has failed. When the Georgia secretary of state unlawfully distributed the personal information of every single Georgia voter, Jen sued -- helping to make sure victims got legal notice and access to credit repair insurance.

**Nikema Williams** (SD-39) is the vice president of public policy for Planned Parenthood Southeast and spends her time fighting for women's health care and reproductive rights. So you can count on her to protect Georgia women and families once she gets to the state legislature.

**Bee Nguyen** (HD-89) is the daughter of immigrants who taught her the critical value of education. She started her own nonprofit to educate and empower girls in underserved communities and is an active fighter in the state for social justice issues.
These women are SO CLOSE to the finish line and are counting on our help to get the rest of the way there.

**Donate $3 to keep the energy from last week going into the Georgia special elections on December 5th and support our candidates across the country who are running for state and local office:**

**[https://secure.emilyslist.org/Win-Georgia-Elections](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c6dfb/24c69fd9/165de43da/1891dcc7/2818168697/VEsF/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/20171117_email_GASpecial?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=3+-+httpssecureemilyslistorgWinGeorgiaElecti&utm_campaign=WQEFEK35N_digital_email_20171118_GASpecial_inact&source=WQEFEK35N_digital_email_20171118_GASpecial_inact")**

Last week, three of our women running in special elections for Georgia state legislative seats earned enough votes to head into runoffs in December. Want to keep adding to our state and local wins this year? "" We've had our eye on the Georgia legislature ever since we met Stacey Abrams, our first-ever Gabrielle Giffords Rising Star Award winner, who we're excited is now running for governor! These women will make us just as proud as Stacey has in the Georgia Capitol: Jen Jordan (SD-06) is a publicly educated lawyer who stands up for the people the government has failed. When the Georgia secretary of state unlawfully distributed the personal information of every single Georgia voter, Jen sued -- helping to make sure victims got legal notice and access to credit repair insurance. Nikema Williams (SD-39) is the vice president of public policy for Planned Parenthood Southeast and spends her time fighting for women's health care and reproductive rights. So you can count on her to protect Georgia women and families once she gets to the state legislature. Bee Nguyen (HD-89) is the daughter of immigrants who taught her the critical value of education. She started her own nonprofit to educate and empower girls in underserved communities and is an active fighter in the state for social justice issues. These women are SO CLOSE to the finish line and are counting on our help to get the rest of the way there. ## [Wisconsin's top Republicans call for Moore to step aside](http://links.iterable.com/u/click?_t=80d255427d5b4d6fa3cb87d23cf0003e&_m=d818a50995204b6396f4a638a13e498d&_e=oVkJ4GC1Txac2qpBhMLksimo9nOILO5FOei9VwGZXqje-yTPKYTkErQ4k1nIo79fqNVIooD8Ma79WhCcYJo2B1lfBhAzKHZuCqNMgr50yLT73cxSmDB33rONPmJ-Q48vq_m72oF5p6ksE6wTiRraPXrmAHJJItbZSkAgwDIeudbtR9gS-joGUJTp4RMQxVILy2G6B4tDP4rxYSf1-WB4-IucN0S5XLN2dxQmuCkTr4SO2CTEty5GAXaRJ2CVrzUiWmz2srLb9nTX-ctC8TSECwQ6ExkbLeMurvaCfptPAfaRMf8G7xJHYzZPVIjCyzC4oF4ausKFvZR_UjxWQ_yLUw%3D%3D)
Republican state Sen. Leah Vukmir, who is running for U.S. Senate next year, said last week that Moore should only leave the race if the allegations were true. Her GOP opponent, Kevin Nicholson, made similar comments. But after another woman came forward Monday to say Moore had assaulted her when she was 16, Vukmir issued a statement Tuesday joining Ryan and Walker in taking a firmer stand. "Roy Moore's conduct as described is very disturbing and should disqualify anyone from serving in public office," Vukmir said in a statement. "The allegations are credible and serious and should be treated as such."
---
|
## [Republicans must rally around conservative reformer Leah Vukmir to oust Baldwin](http://links.iterable.com/u/click?_t=80d255427d5b4d6fa3cb87d23cf0003e&_m=d818a50995204b6396f4a638a13e498d&_e=omM0XYP3E0Im4GXhdoPP4lH813kbL2GqPBTcq_38qd3GRJhq9cFZX2SsydGVYPoMeMz8n60Agt7lEf6zZfllTSwxWLVa6AsyYbSsrOq5bBfk8Jb0yuhpFnSZHc5Thu3s_Yi_LrfQILmhC1D3_pKrWA9Cyl-6VrRN5jh3gmH_mrwDq7Lnvvm7nq9mi5hA7Glob-F9nXau71XqyrZieJ4skMR7vZNOuXp2Jo6RXac1EUCTeGSatpMT1b-GO8jU0tnKtNfw_oqZVFHckRqTzh-vCDmxJ65I4MVvKM25xytKCia0G5ulk3vMniCTWPp6oajoYOb5DZLk6CuMSxiajowzWiCa7Y10lh8KRlrsjtaorjMsDrfnBgtTlo9MTu41BeOpnT7jTuq02Dc45F-dSb_8bg%3D%3D)
Leah Vukmir has a spectacular record of supporting conservative reforms. She has been a phenomenal senator at the state level and I expect the same if she is elected to serve as senator at the federal level.... She stood by Walker during the political battles over Act 10. She has consistently voted for tax reductions to grow the Wisconsin economy. She has been a proponent of lifting the cap on Milwaukee parental choice for school vouchers. She has supported policies like right-to-work and voter ID. She is robustly pro-life. She has been fighting for law and order to protect the Wisconsin citizenry. Stances like these are why Vukmir has amassed over 100 endorsements in a short period of time. Currently, it has been difficult for the Republicans in the U.S. Senate to pass their largest legislation. There’s a slim majority of two, and the repeal of Obamacare was halted by just three Republican senators. Had the majority been greater, the repealing and replacement of the health care law would’ve been successful. In times like these, it would be ideal to have someone like Leah Vukmir in the U.S. Senate.
---
|
## [Sen. Leah Vukmir encourages students to seek bipartisan friendships during visit to College Republicans](http://links.iterable.com/u/click?_t=80d255427d5b4d6fa3cb87d23cf0003e&_m=d818a50995204b6396f4a638a13e498d&_e=omM0XYP3E0Im4GXhdoPP4lH813kbL2GqPBTcq_38qd2JPvw4W8ToFtDTbvKTp-y2h6kJ88JFLzQuU5BVo2416uZn22nXNEB8jCTRpjbLIeVm_Qa3t1gXpxianhEXNmXlXsYp3HxH_CH3oN0n9qN_wYb-qkKwZLucO8ARCC5tFs_WUy1MGgWTaw8TLd0WFn015gVAqGmxNeuNxJ_zphxXcunbA_agiMFhUAkPRp2YKorvMIEHjwqMZD5rv0T965PzEwbjOvjs1HRqN4-2EGiKNstuwdae8davmnKJ1dgKeBnT3YGsgUROLHEhI9O2Pqx-DL6mUjKgcptCssaJBBa98tU-VwR_J8bs3dXt95wBNHI4apYqSFvaQAo-pyk3hmXgjF8i6TN1DIMPIiWV5Eo56A2YoJsGbHFG2AZuJcnb108%3D)
Vukmir encouraged students to get to know people on the other side of the political spectrum as individuals because those friendships can be crucial not only in politics, but to becoming a well-rounded person. Talking to people who disagree with you can be intense, as it is in the state Senate sometimes, but having those friendships will pay off down the road, Vukmir said. “You would never know that we get things done on a bipartisan basis because the media doesn’t want to talk about it,” Vukmir said.

clean_body: Happy opening weekend of deer hunting season! Leah, and the rest of us on her team, would like to give our best wishes for all of those out hunting | | | | | | | | | Nov. 18, 2017 Happy opening weekend of deer hunting season! Leah, and the rest of us on her team, would like to give our best wishes for all of those out hunting this weekend. Stay safe! We'd especially like to wish good luck to those kids under 10, who , are out deer hunting with their parents for the first time. This week, Leah has been busy traveling the state and talking to voters about why she is the best candidate to take on Tammy Baldwin next fall. She was up in and on Sunday, and on Wednesday, and she visited and on Thursday for Pints and Politics events. Leah also spent a lot of time this week talking to College Republicans across the state. She went to four different colleges during the week — , , and . While there, she talked to the young conservative leaders about the Senate race and the importance of electing a proven, consistent leader in 2018. Thank you to everyone who has come out to these events to rally behind Leah as we look to take the Wisconsin Way to Washington! As always, keep up with Leah on the campaign trail by following her , and pages. Happy deer hunting! Team Leah | News that drove the week | Republican state Sen. Leah Vukmir, who is running for U.S. Senate next year, said last week that Moore should only leave the race if the allegations were true. Her GOP opponent, Kevin Nicholson, made similar comments. But after another woman came forward Monday to say Moore had assaulted her when she was 16, Vukmir issued a statement Tuesday joining Ryan and Walker in taking a firmer stand. "Roy Moore's conduct as described is very disturbing and should disqualify anyone from serving in public office," Vukmir said in a statement. "The allegations are credible and serious and should be treated as such." | Leah Vukmir has a spectacular record of supporting conservative reforms. She has been a phenomenal senator at the state level and I expect the same if she is elected to serve as senator at the federal level.... She stood by Walker during the political battles over Act 10. She has consistently voted for tax reductions to grow the Wisconsin economy. She has been a proponent of lifting the cap on Milwaukee parental choice for school vouchers. She has supported policies like right-to-work and voter ID. She is robustly pro-life. She has been fighting for law and order to protect the Wisconsin citizenry. Stances like these are why Vukmir has amassed over 100 endorsements in a short period of time. Currently, it has been difficult for the Republicans in the U.S. Senate to pass their largest legislation. There’s a slim majority of two, and the repeal of Obamacare was halted by just three Republican senators. Had the majority been greater, the repealing and replacement of the health care law would’ve been successful. In times like these, it would be ideal to have someone like Leah Vukmir in the U.S. Senate. | Vukmir encouraged students to get to know people on the other side of the political spectrum as individuals because those friendships can be crucial not only in politics, but to becoming a well-rounded person. Talking to people who disagree with you can be intense, as it is in the state Senate sometimes, but having those friendships will pay off down the road, Vukmir said. “You would never know that we get things done on a bipartisan basis because the media doesn’t want to talk about it,” Vukmir said. | Leah on social media | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | Paid for by Leah for Senate

We need clean energy and international cooperation, and we need it now. We've already gathered over 5,000 names, but we're missing yours, friend. [**Sign on now to tell Donald Trump and other climate deniers -- they can 't ignore science.**](http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=2&ms=MTIzODg3MTcS1&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=0&j=MTI4MTY4MjkzMQS2&mt=1&rt=0) Very truly yours, Jay Inslee

From: Jay Inslee
Date: Wednesday, Nov 15, 2017
Subject: You'll never guess who Trump sent to Bonn. Friend, Donald Trump's contribution to the UN Climate Conference in Bonn, Germany? A pro-coal panel led by fossil fuel promoters. At a _climate change_ conference. So we showed up to keep them honest. Oregon Gov. Kate Brown and I brought a clear message to the press at their panel: While Trump denies climate change, we are moving forward to a clean energy future. On the West Coast, we're building a Great Blue Wall that puts people and our climate ahead of the big-coal and big-oil lobby. [**Join us: Add your name to speak out against Donald Trump 's denial of climate science.**](http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=2&ms=MTIzODg3MTcS1&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=0&j=MTI4MTY4MjkzMQS2&mt=1&rt=0) [](http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=2&ms=MTIzODg3MTcS1&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=0&j=MTI4MTY4MjkzMQS2&mt=1&rt=0) Every single person I talked to in Bonn was more energized than ever to take on climate action. It's a shame that the White House isn't harnessing that energy -- but where they fall short in leadership, we'll stand up to prove our strength. When confronted with anti-science lies that threaten our world's future, it's our moral responsibility to speak the truth, loudly and forcefully. And it's our responsibility to act. That's what we did in Bonn. That's what we're doing in Washington state -- and across the country with the U.S. Climate Alliance, a partnership with 14 of my fellow governors collectively representing almost 40% of the U.S. economy. [**So stand with us, and stand with the world: Sign on now to reject Donald Trump 's denial of climate science through action.**](http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=2&ms=MTIzODg3MTcS1&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=0&j=MTI4MTY4MjkzMQS2&mt=1&rt=0) Donald Trump can't stop us. name: Chris Van Hollen
subject: together we can defeat this
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Van Hollen for US Senate
body:
| __ What is happening is totally outrageous. This week, Republicans in the House of Representatives passed a tax bill that would dramatically increase income inequality in America by giving the already wealthy a big tax cut that is financed by increasing taxes on millions of middle class families, leaving our children and grandchildren to pay-off a whopping $1.5 trillion increase in the national debt.

So it's hard to believe that the Republicans on the Senate Finance Committee could pass something even worse -- but they did. First, they eliminated entirely the ability for people to deduct their state and local taxes. As a result, millions of middle class families will see their taxes go up to pay for tax giveaways to big corporations. Second, they repealed the provision in the Affordable Care Act that requires everyone to take some responsibility for their own health care coverage. Eliminating that provision will spike premiums by more than 10% and result in 13 million fewer Americans with affordable health coverage.

[**We cannot afford another "faith-based" trickle-down bill built around wishful thinking that a massive giveaway to large corporations and the wealthiest will eventually help everyone else. I will be doing everything I possibly can to stop this tax bill, but I need you to be engaged in this battle too. Sign here to stand with me against this outrageous bill. >>**](http://action.vanhollen.org/page/m/2de91bb9/70cdc2ba/46cee98b/3902e26/3727966855/VEsH/ "http://action.vanhollen.org/page/s/stop-republican-giveaways?utm_medium=email&utm_source=cvanhollen&utm_content=1+-+We+cannot+afford+another+faithbased+tric&utm_campaign=em111817&source=em111817")

The truth is that there are many common sense measures we could take to reform our tax code in a bipartisan way. But Republicans in Congress are not interested in talking. They want to ram through their corporate giveaways before the public has time to see what's in their bill.

I know tax policy can be dry at times, but we can't afford to sit out this debate. These Republican tax plans, if passed, will do severe damage to our country for decades to come.

[**We defeated the effort to destroy the ACA because people rose up in protest. We need you again. Please reach out to people you know around the country and urge them to get engaged in this fight. We need you!**](http://action.vanhollen.org/page/m/2de91bb9/70cdc2ba/46cee98b/3902e27/3727966855/VEsE/ "http://action.vanhollen.org/page/s/stop-republican-giveaways?utm_medium=email&utm_source=cvanhollen&utm_content=2+-+We+defeated+the+effort+to+destroy+the+AC&utm_campaign=em111817&source=em111817")

This is likely to come down to one vote in the Senate. If we all stand together, we can defeat this bill.

Thank you for staying engaged in the fight!

Chris Van Hollen

U.S. Senator
